reasoning: The forecast horizon is extremely short (approx. 33 days between the knowledge cutoff of May 29, 2025, and the July 1, 2025, deadline). While Senator Fetterman is facing significant political and personal blowback following a scathing New York Magazine profile in early May, his response has been wholly defiant. He has called the article a "hit piece," explicitly stated he is in "good health," and declared he will not resign or switch parties, intending to serve his full term until 2029. Furthermore, despite reports of him leaving a Democratic caucus group chat and being courted by Republicans, Democratic leadership such as Chuck Schumer continue to defend him publicly as a "good legislator," indicating no formal party mechanism is mobilizing to expel him quickly. Given the very high bar and typically lengthy timeline for a sitting Senator to resign or change caucuses—especially when actively resisting such calls—the only plausible path to a 'Yes' resolution within 33 days is a sudden, extreme deterioration in his health that forces an immediate departure. This represents a very small tail risk.
